1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with bladder cancer — malignant neoplasm of the urinary bladder. More common in males due to smoking and occupational exposures.

1-expert.prompt_text: I need to find patients with a documented bladder cancer diagnosis from their clinical records. Search Condition resources for malignant neoplasm of the urinary bladder coded in SNOMED CT, as recorded on the problem list or as an encounter diagnosis.

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients with bladder cancer using common code systems:

**Using SNOMED CT:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363346000&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Using ICD-10-CM (general bladder malignancy):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C67.9&_include=Condition:patient
```

**Using ICD-10-CM (broader bladder cancer category with prefix search):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C67&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Code References:
| System | Code | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| SNOMED CT | `363346000` | Malignant neoplasm of urinary bladder |
| ICD-10-CM | `C67.9` | Malignant neoplasm of bladder, unspecified |
| ICD-10-CM | `C67` | Malignant neoplasm of bladder (parent category covering C67.0–C67.9) |

> **Note:** `_include=Condition:patient` retrieves the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle. Run all three queries or combine code values (e.g., `code=system|C67.0,system|C67.1,...`) to ensure full coverage across coding systems used in your FHIR server.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Bladder Cancer — Malignant Neoplasm of Urinary Bladder

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C67.9&_include=Condition:subject&clinical-status=active

Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C67&_include=Condition:subject

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363416001&_include=Condition:subject

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|188527007&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🔍 Query Rationale

| Query | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ICD-10-CM exact | `C67.9` | Malignant neoplasm of bladder, unspecified |
| ICD-10-CM `:below` | `C67` | Captures **all subtypes**: C67.0–C67.9 (trigone, dome, lateral/anterior/posterior wall, neck, ureteric orifice, urachus, overlapping) |
| SNOMED CT | `363416001` | Malignant tumor of urinary bladder (disorder) |
| SNOMED CT | `188527007` | Malignant neoplasm of urinary bladder |

---

### 📋 Clinical Context
- **ICD-10 parent**: `C67` covers all anatomical subsites of the urinary bladder
- Use **`:below`** modifier to sweep all sub-coded variants in a single query
- `_include=Condition:subject` retrieves linked **Patient** resources in one call
- Filter by `clinical-status=active` to focus on current diagnoses vs. historical records
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with bladder cancer — malignant neoplasm of the urinary bladder. More common in males due to smoking and occupational exposures.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363423001&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&verification-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-ver-status|confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363423001&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|encounter-diagnosis&verification-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-ver-status|confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | `http://snomed.info/sct\|363423001` | SNOMED CT: *Malignant neoplasm of urinary bladder (disorder)* |
| `category` (Query 1) | `…\|problem-list-item` | Diagnoses recorded on the active problem list |
| `category` (Query 2) | `…\|encounter-diagnosis` | Diagnoses recorded as part of a clinical encounter |
| `verification-status` | `…\|confirmed` | Limits to clinically confirmed diagnoses (excludes suspected/entered-in-error) |
| `_include=Condition:subject` | — | Pulls associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle |

### Notes
- **Two queries are used** to separately target both clinical documentation contexts (problem list vs. encounter diagnosis), as different EHR systems may populate only one or the other.
- If your server supports comma-separated OR logic for `category`, these can be combined into a single query:
  ```
  Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363423001&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item,http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|encounter-diagnosis&verification-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-ver-status|confirmed&_include=Condition:subject
  ```
- Consider also searching with ICD-10-CM `C67` codes if your system dual-codes diagnoses.

2-broad.raw_response:
149 total — stable count confirmed. All records are coded with SNOMED `399326009`; ICD-10-CM adds 0 additional patients on this server. Here is the complete picture of what was found:

---

### 🔍 Investigation Summary

| Step | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4, Azure — SNOMED-only Condition codings (no ICD-10-CM populated) |
| **Sampled Conditions (3/3)** | All carried `SNOMED 399326009` — "Malignant neoplasm of urinary bladder" |
| **SNOMED variants tested** | `363455001`, `93689003`, `188248005`, `154540000`, `269607003` → all returned **0** on this server |
| **ICD-10-CM (VSAC value set)** | `C67.0–C67.9` (10 site-specific subcodes from *Bladder Cancer for Urology Care* OID `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1248.354`) → returned **0** on this server |
| **Final count** | **149 Condition records** across all code systems combined |

### Code Strategy
- **Primary driver:** SNOMED `399326009` captures 100% of this server's data
- **ICD-10-CM `C67.0–C67.9`** included for portability (site-specific subtypes: trigone, dome, lateral/anterior/posterior wall, bladder neck, ureteric orifice, urachus, overlapping, unspecified)
- **`93689003`** (Primary malignant neoplasm) and **`188248005`** (Bladder Ca NOS) added as synonym guards
